prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1) English
Welcome aboard — it’s great to have you on the team; I’m looking forward to working with you.
Note: Warm, professionally informal — first-name basis and friendly small talk are common in many English-speaking workplaces.

2) Spanish
¡Mucho gusto en conocerte! Espero que trabajemos muy bien juntos.
Note: Uses the informal "tú" form—friendly and collegial; in some Spanish-speaking regions you'd use "usted" with seniors or to show extra respect.

3) French
Enchanté(e) de faire votre connaissance ! Au plaisir de travailler ensemble.
Note: Uses the formal "vous" — respectful and typical on first meetings at work unless someone invites you to use "tu"; "Enchanté/Enchantée" varies by speaker gender.

4) German
Schön, dich kennenzulernen! Ich freue mich auf die Zusammenarbeit.
Note: Informal "du" implies closeness and is increasingly common among peers; using "Sie" would signal formality and distance.

5) Japanese
はじめまして。これからどうぞよろしくお願いします。 (Hajimemashite. Korekara dōzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu.)
Note: Polite and formal—appropriate for first introductions; using the family name + さん and observing hierarchical respect is culturally important.
